title: Roast Beef with Peppery Dutch Peas
description: A hearty roast beef dish with a pop of peppery flavor from the Dutch peas. Perfect for a cold winter's night.

ingredients.name: Roast beef (2 pounds)
ingredients.notes: Choose a high-quality cut with good marbling for maximum flavor.

ingredients.name: Dutch peas
ingredients.notes: Frozen peas are fine too, but fresh are better.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons butter
ingredients.notes: Unsalted is best, but salted will do in a pinch.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon dried thyme
ingredients.notes: You can use fresh thyme too, if you like.

ingredients.name: 1 cup beef broth
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium if you can, or omit the salt altogether.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Not too much, mind you! This is a beef dish, after all.

instructions: Season the roast beef with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat the butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the roast beef until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the roast beef to a large plate and set aside.
Add the Dutch peas, thyme, and beef broth to the Dutch oven. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Return the roast beef to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and roast for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the beef reaches an internal temperature of 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the roast beef rest for 15-20 minutes before slicing and serving.
Strain the cooking liquid and discard the thyme. Serve the roast beef with the Dutch peas and a drizzle of the cooking liquid.
